reason: Core Pydantic data models and enums shared by all recruiting modules — defines the canonical schema for candidates, conversations, pipeline stages, and scoring
description: Pydantic v2 models for all Firestore document types: CandidateDocument (30+ fields covering identity, academic, screening, pipeline, call tracking, homework, trial data), ConversationDocument (messages array, screening signals, session metadata), HandshakeSyncState (polling watermark), RecruitingConfig (parsed YAML runtime config). Enums for PipelineStage (ingested, registered, screening, scored, call_queued, called, homework, trial, hired, rejected), CallOutcome, HomeworkStatus, TrialStatus. Includes Firestore serialization helpers (to_dict/from_dict) and validation rules. All models use strict mode with field validators for email, phone, URL formats.

context_urls.purpose: Pydantic v2 model definition patterns and validation
Field validators for email, phone, URL format enforcement
Firestore document structure and data types

reason: YAML configuration loader for chatbot system prompt, screening criteria, scoring weights, filter rules, and Brevo template IDs — single source of truth for all tunable recruiting parameters
description: Loads and validates extensions/recruiting/config.yaml into a RecruitingConfig Pydantic model. Provides a cached singleton accessor get_config() that reads YAML once and returns validated config. Handles missing file with sensible defaults, validates scoring weight totals sum to 1.0, and exposes typed accessors for chatbot_prompt, screening_criteria, scoring_weights, filter_rules, brevo_template_ids, and scheduling config. Supports config reload for testing.

context_urls.purpose: Cached singleton pattern for config loading
YAML parsing with safe_load

functions.name: get_config
functions.signature: def get_config(config_path: str | None = None) -> RecruitingConfig

reason: Shared authentication utilities for three distinct auth patterns used across recruiting endpoints — GitHub OAuth token exchange, Firebase ID token verification, and RS256 signed JWT for email deep-links
description: Three auth patterns: (1) verify_firebase_token(token) validates Firebase ID tokens via firebase_admin.auth.verify_id_token(), returns uid and claims, handles expired/invalid/revoked tokens. (2) verify_github_oauth(code) exchanges GitHub OAuth code for access token, fetches user profile (login, email, avatar_url, name), returns GitHubProfile. (3) create_signed_jwt(payload, expiry) and verify_signed_jwt(token) use RS256 JWT signing/verification via PyJWT for call outcome deep-links with configurable secret from environment. All functions raise typed AuthError exceptions with error codes. Includes helper for extracting Bearer tokens from Authorization headers.

context_urls.purpose: Firebase Admin SDK token verification with verify_id_token()
GitHub OAuth code-to-token exchange flow
PyJWT RS256 signing and verification patterns

functions.name: create_signed_jwt
functions.signature: def create_signed_jwt(payload: dict, expiry_hours: int = 72) -> str

reason: Handshake API polling function triggered hourly by Cloud Scheduler — ingests new applicants, de-duplicates against existing candidates, applies auto-filter rules, and creates candidate documents
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_handshake_poll triggered by Cloud Scheduler (hourly). Fetches applicants from Handshake EDU API using updated_since watermark from recruiting/handshake_sync_state document. For each applicant: extracts identity and academic fields, checks for duplicates by email in recruiting/candidates collection, applies configurable auto-filter rules (GPA threshold, graduation year, major whitelist from config.yaml), creates CandidateDocument with stage=ingested for passing applicants. Updates sync watermark on success. Handles API rate limits with exponential backoff, logs filtered-out candidates with rejection reasons, implements idempotency via Handshake application ID. Uses structured Cloud Logging.

context_urls.purpose: Handshake EDU API endpoints and authentication
Cloud Functions HTTP function pattern for scheduler triggers
Firestore transactions for atomic watermark updates

endpoints.responseSchema: { ingested: int, filtered: int, duplicates: int }

reason: Candidate registration endpoint handling GitHub OAuth flow — creates Firebase Auth user, candidate document, and waitlist entry in a single atomic operation
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_register triggered by HTTP POST. Accepts GitHub OAuth authorization code, exchanges for access token via auth_utils, creates or links Firebase Auth account with GitHub provider, creates CandidateDocument with stage=registered populated from GitHub profile (login, email, avatar_url, name). Creates user_waitlist entry with source='recruiting' and recruiting_uid reference. Returns Firebase custom token for client-side auth. Handles duplicate registration (same GitHub user) by returning existing candidate. Uses Firestore batch write for atomicity across candidates, waitlist, and users collections. Validates request payload with Pydantic.

context_urls.purpose: Creating Firebase custom tokens for client auth
Batch writes for atomic multi-document creation
GitHub user profile API for candidate data

endpoints.responseSchema: { uid: str, token: str, candidate: CandidateDocument }

reason: AI chatbot endpoint for conversational screening — manages multi-turn conversations with Claude via LiteLLM, extracts screening signals, and persists chat history
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_chat triggered by HTTP POST with Firebase token auth. Manages multi-turn conversation flow: loads conversation history from recruiting/conversations/{uid}, appends user message, constructs system prompt from config.yaml with candidate context, calls Claude via LiteLLM (model from config, temperature=0.7), extracts screening signals from assistant response (technical depth, communication quality, motivation indicators), persists updated conversation and signals to Firestore. Handles conversation lifecycle: creates new conversation on first message, enforces max turns from config, marks conversation status=complete when screening criteria met or max turns reached. On completion, updates candidate stage to screening complete and triggers score_candidate via Firestore write. Returns assistant message and conversation metadata. Implements request-level idempotency via message_id.

context_urls.purpose: LiteLLM unified API for Claude model calls
Claude message format and multi-turn conversation patterns
Firestore document updates for conversation persistence

endpoints.responseSchema: { reply: str, conversation_status: str, turn_number: int }

reason: Candidate scoring triggered by conversation completion — computes weighted composite score from screening signals and generates candidate dossier for reviewer
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_score_candidate triggered by Firestore document update on recruiting/conversations/{uid} when status changes to complete. Reads screening signals from conversation document, loads scoring weights from config.yaml (technical_depth, communication, motivation, experience, culture_fit), computes weighted composite score (0-100). Generates text dossier summarizing candidate strengths, concerns, and recommended next steps using LiteLLM. Updates CandidateDocument with composite_score, individual dimension scores, dossier text, and advances stage to scored. Handles at-least-once delivery via idempotency check on candidate stage. Logs scoring breakdown for audit trail.

context_urls.purpose: Firestore trigger patterns for Cloud Functions 2nd gen
LiteLLM completion API for dossier generation

sideEffects: Updates candidate document with score and dossier

functions.signature: async def generate_dossier(candidate: CandidateDocument, signals: ScreeningSignals, scores: dict) -> str

reason: Shared email outreach module using Brevo transactional API — sends templated emails for post-screen notifications, homework instructions, and other candidate communications
description: Brevo transactional email integration module. Provides send_email(to, template_id, params) using Brevo Python SDK v4.0. Template IDs loaded from config.yaml for each email type: post_screen_notification, homework_instructions, call_invitation, rejection_notice. Handles Brevo API errors with retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff). Validates email addresses before sending. Logs all email sends with template_id and recipient for audit. Provides bulk_send for daily digest. All functions are async for non-blocking operation within Cloud Functions.

context_urls.purpose: Brevo transactional email API reference

reason: Daily digest Cloud Scheduler function — generates ranked call queue with dossiers, phone numbers, and email templates for the hiring manager
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_daily_digest triggered by Cloud Scheduler at 4 PM PT daily. Queries Firestore for all candidates with stage=scored or stage=call_queued, ranks by composite_score descending, generates HTML email with: ranked table (name, score, phone, key strengths), individual dossier summaries, pre-filled email template links for each candidate, and signed JWT deep-links for call outcome tracking. Sends digest via email_outreach.send_bulk_digest to configured admin email. Updates candidates to stage=call_queued if not already. Handles empty queue gracefully (sends no-candidates summary). Uses config.yaml for admin_email, digest schedule, and email template.

context_urls.purpose: Cloud Scheduler job configuration for daily triggers
Firestore compound queries for candidate filtering and ordering

reason: Call outcome tracking endpoint — records hiring manager call results via email deep-link with signed JWT authentication, triggers downstream pipeline transitions
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_call_outcome triggered by HTTP POST. Accepts signed JWT (from daily digest deep-link) or admin Firebase token auth. Validates JWT payload contains candidate_uid, extracts call outcome (interested, not_interested, no_answer, callback_requested, hired, rejected) and optional notes. Updates CandidateDocument with call outcome, call timestamp, and notes. Advances pipeline stage based on outcome: interested to homework, hired to hired, rejected to rejected, no_answer remains call_queued, callback_requested remains call_queued with callback_date. Triggers downstream effects: homework stage triggers send_homework via Firestore write. Returns updated candidate status. Implements idempotency via call_outcome_id in JWT payload.

context_urls.purpose: RS256 JWT verification for deep-link authentication
Firestore atomic field updates for call outcome recording

endpoints.requestSchema: { token: str, outcome: str, notes: str? }
endpoints.responseSchema: { uid: str, stage: str, outcome: str }

reason: Homework assignment pipeline triggered by candidate stage transition — auto-approves waitlist, grants credits, and sends homework instructions
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_send_homework triggered by Firestore document update on recruiting/candidates/{uid} when stage changes to homework. Performs three atomic operations: (1) updates user_waitlist entry to approved=true for the candidate waitlist doc, (2) grants homework_credits to users/{uid} document (credit amount from config.yaml), (3) sends homework instruction email via email_outreach with assignment details, deadline, and submission instructions. Updates CandidateDocument with homework_assigned_at timestamp, homework_deadline, and homework_status=assigned. Handles at-least-once delivery via idempotency check on homework_status. Uses Firestore batch write for atomicity across three collections.

context_urls.purpose: Firestore trigger for stage transition detection
Batch writes for atomic cross-collection updates

functions.name: grant_homework_credits
functions.signature: def grant_homework_credits(uid: str, credits: int) -> None

reason: Trial tracking module for monitoring candidate GitHub contributions during trial period — tracks status transitions and contribution metrics
description: Provides functions for trial lifecycle management. start_trial(uid) sets trial_start_date, trial_end_date (from config duration), and trial_status=active on CandidateDocument. check_trial_contributions(uid) queries GitHub REST API for candidate recent contributions (commits, PRs, issues) in the organization repositories, computes contribution metrics (commit_count, pr_count, lines_added, lines_removed, review_comments). update_trial_status(uid) evaluates contributions against config thresholds and updates trial_status (active to completed, extended, or failed). Can be called by a scheduled function or admin endpoint. Handles GitHub API rate limits, caches API responses, and logs contribution details. Uses candidate GitHub login from CandidateDocument for API queries.

context_urls.purpose: GitHub REST API for listing commits by author
GitHub REST API for listing PRs by author
GitHub API rate limit handling

functions.name: start_trial
sideEffects: Sets trial dates and status on candidate document

functions.name: update_trial_status
sideEffects: Updates candidate document trial_status and stage

reason: Shared Pydantic models for CRM nurture pipeline — defines CrmFunnelStage, ContributionSummary, MergedPR, ResurfaceReason enums and 7 module-local types used across nurture, hackathon, and resurface modules
description: Pydantic v2 models extending the core candidate_models for nurture pipeline concerns. Shared types: CrmFunnelStage enum (nurture, hackathon_invited, contributing, resurfaced, churned), ContributionSummary (merged_pr_count, hackathon_participations, platform_usage_days, marketplace_examples, total_lines_added), MergedPR (repo, pr_number, title, merged_at, lines_added, lines_removed), ResurfaceReason enum (merged_prs, hackathon_score, power_user, marketplace_contributor). Module-local types: NurtureEmailAction enum (recruiting-welcome, recruiting-contribute-invite, recruiting-hackathon-invite), WebhookPayload/PRWebhookData for GitHub webhook parsing, HackathonInviteContext for invite template params, ResurfaceResult for daily scan output, DigestCandidate for weekly digest rendering, HackathonRegistrationFix for user_id normalization. All models include Firestore serialization helpers consistent with candidate_models.

context_urls.purpose: Pydantic v2 model definition and BaseModel patterns
Pydantic model configuration and strict mode
Enum and standard type handling in Pydantic v2

reason: CRM contact creation and nurture email pipeline — Firestore trigger that auto-approves waitlist, grants starter credits, creates GitHub Issue CRM contact, and sends nurture welcome email
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_route_to_nurture triggered by Firestore document update on recruiting/candidates/{uid} when call_outcome is set to 'nurture' (routed from call_tracking). Performs four operations atomically: (1) creates GitHub Issue as CRM contact with recruiting-nurture and recruiting-candidate labels via GitHub Issues API, stores crmIssueNumber on candidate doc, (2) updates user_waitlist/{uid} to approved=true for auto-approval, (3) grants 10 PDDC starter credits to users/{uid} via Firestore increment, (4) sends recruiting-welcome nurture email via email_outreach with Brevo template. Updates CrmFunnelStage to 'nurture' and records nurture_emails_sent=['recruiting-welcome']. Subsequent nurture emails (recruiting-contribute-invite, recruiting-hackathon-invite) are sent by separate scheduled checks or downstream triggers. Implements idempotency via crmIssueNumber existence check to prevent duplicate CRM contacts. Uses Firestore batch write for atomicity across candidates, waitlist, and users collections.

context_urls.purpose: Firestore trigger patterns for route-to-nurture function
GitHub Issues API for CRM contact creation
GitHub Issues label management for recruiting labels
Firestore batch writes for atomic waitlist approval and credit granting
Brevo transactional email for nurture welcome sequence

reason: GitHub PR webhook handler for tracking candidate contributions — updates ContributionSummary on merge events for nurtured candidates
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_contribution_webhook triggered by HTTP POST from GitHub webhook (pull_request event). Validates webhook signature using HMAC SHA-256 with configured webhook secret. Parses PRWebhookData from payload, filters for action='closed' and merged=true events only. Looks up candidate by GitHub login in recruiting/candidates collection. If candidate found and in nurture funnel: creates MergedPR record, increments ContributionSummary.merged_pr_count, appends PR to ContributionSummary.merged_prs array, updates last_activity_at timestamp. Also handles platform_usage and marketplace_examples tracking via separate webhook events or periodic polling. Sends recruiting-contribute-invite nurture email after first contribution if not already sent. Returns 200 for valid webhooks, 401 for signature failures, 204 for non-matching events. Implements idempotency via PR number + repo deduplication.

context_urls.purpose: GitHub webhook pull_request event payload format
Webhook HMAC SHA-256 signature verification
GitHub REST API for listing PRs by author
Firestore document updates for contribution aggregation

reason: Hackathon invite trigger — Firestore onUpdate trigger that sends hackathon invitations to nurtured candidates when hackathon events open for registration
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_hackathon_invite_trigger triggered by Firestore document update on hackathon_events/{event_id} when status changes to 'open'. Queries recruiting/candidates for all candidates with crmFunnelStage in ('nurture', 'contributing') who have not yet received a hackathon invite (hackathon_invite_sent != true). For each eligible candidate: sends recruiting-hackathon-invite nurture email via Brevo with HackathonInviteContext (event name, dates, registration URL), updates hackathon_invite_sent=true and appends 'recruiting-hackathon-invite' to nurture_emails_sent array. Uses Firestore batch writes to update candidates in batches of 500 (Firestore limit). Implements re-trigger loop prevention by checking that the event status actually changed from non-open to open (compares before/after snapshots). Logs invite count and any email delivery failures.

context_urls.purpose: Firestore onUpdate trigger for hackathon_events collection
Cloud Functions Firestore trigger configuration
Brevo email for hackathon invite delivery
Firestore queries for candidate nurture stage filtering

reason: Hackathon score sync — Firestore onUpdate trigger that syncs hackathon submission scores back to recruiting candidate documents and applies user_id field naming normalization bug fix
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_hackathon_sync triggered by Firestore document update on hackathon_submissions/{submission_id} when compliance_score or final_score fields are updated. Looks up the submitting user via user_id field (applying userId -> user_id normalization fix), cross-references with recruiting/candidates by uid. Updates candidate document with hackathon_score (max of compliance + final scores across submissions), increments ContributionSummary.hackathon_participations, and updates last_activity_at. Also handles hackathon_registrations user_id normalization: on first run, scans for documents with 'userId' field and rewrites to 'user_id' for consistency. Implements idempotency via score comparison (only updates if new score > existing). Uses Firestore transactions for atomic read-modify-write on candidate document to prevent lost updates from concurrent submissions.

context_urls.purpose: Firestore onUpdate trigger for hackathon_submissions
Firestore field updates for score sync and user_id normalization
Batch writes for atomic score sync across collections

sideEffects: Normalizes user_id field naming in registrations

reason: Daily Rising Stars resurface check — Cloud Scheduler function scanning contribution thresholds to identify high-potential nurtured candidates and detect churned candidates
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_resurface_check triggered by Cloud Scheduler (daily). Scans all recruiting/candidates with crmFunnelStage in ('nurture', 'contributing'). For each candidate, evaluates four resurface criteria from config thresholds: (1) merged_prs >= threshold (e.g., 3 merged PRs), (2) hackathon_score >= threshold (e.g., top 25%), (3) platform_usage_days >= threshold (power user), (4) marketplace_examples >= threshold (marketplace contributor). Candidates meeting any criterion are marked resurfaced=true with appropriate ResurfaceReason, CrmFunnelStage updated to 'resurfaced', and CRM GitHub Issue label updated from recruiting-nurture to recruiting-resurfaced. Also detects churned candidates: if last_activity_at > 60 days ago and no contributions, sets crmFunnelStage='churned' and updates CRM label to recruiting-churned. Verifies merged PR counts via GitHub API to prevent gaming. Logs all resurface and churn transitions. Returns summary counts.

context_urls.purpose: Cloud Scheduler daily job configuration
Firestore compound queries for contribution threshold scanning
GitHub API for merged PR count verification
Cloud Functions HTTP entry point for scheduler triggers

endpoints.responseSchema: { scanned: int, resurfaced: int, churned: int, reasons: dict }

reason: Weekly Rising Stars digest email — Cloud Scheduler function aggregating resurfaced candidates into an HTML summary email sent to the hiring manager every Monday
description: Cloud Function entry point recruiting_rising_stars_digest triggered by Cloud Scheduler (weekly, Monday 8 AM PT). Queries recruiting/candidates where resurfaced=true and crmFunnelStage='resurfaced', ordered by resurface timestamp descending. For each candidate, creates DigestCandidate with name, GitHub login, resurface reason, contribution summary metrics, hackathon scores, and CRM issue link. Generates HTML digest email using Python string.Template with sections: new Rising Stars since last digest, contribution highlights, hackathon performance, and recommended next steps. Sends digest via email_outreach.send_bulk_digest to configured admin_email (Greg). Marks digested candidates with last_digest_sent_at timestamp to avoid duplicate reporting. Handles empty list gracefully (skips sending). Includes links to individual CRM GitHub Issues for quick review.

context_urls.purpose: Cloud Scheduler weekly job configuration (Monday 8 AM PT)
Brevo API for HTML digest email delivery
Firestore queries for resurfaced candidate aggregation
Python string templates for HTML digest generation
